title: Vector Database Retrieval
summary: Vector search is a recall pipeline that still needs filtering, ranking, and operational guardrails.
task: Explain when a vector database helps and what production tradeoffs matter.
constraints: Embeddings are approximate semantic representations, not exact truth
ANN indexes trade recall, latency, memory, and rebuild cost
Many products need metadata filters and permission checks before results are shown

blocks.title: Embedding model defines meaning
blocks.body: Documents and queries are converted into vectors by the same embedding model. Model choice determines what semantic similarity actually means for the product.

blocks.title: ANN index narrows candidates
blocks.body: The vector index finds approximate nearest neighbors quickly. Parameters tune latency versus recall, so exact correctness is not the default behavior.

blocks.title: Metadata filters protect semantics
blocks.body: Tenant id, language, document type, freshness, and permissions often filter candidates before or after vector lookup. The filter strategy affects both latency and correctness.

blocks.title: Reranking improves precision
blocks.body: A second-stage ranker can score the top candidates using richer context. This is slower, but usually improves answer quality more than increasing raw top-k.

blocks.title: Operations are part of the design
blocks.body: Embedding backfills, index rebuilds, stale vectors, and offline evaluation all matter. Without measurement, vector search can feel impressive while quietly returning poor results.

callouts.title: Interview-ready caveat
callouts.body: A vector DB is not a magic database replacement; it is one retrieval stage in a measured ranking pipeline.

talk_track: I would frame vector databases as a retrieval tool, not a source of truth. The important design is the pipeline: create embeddings, use an ANN index to get candidates, apply metadata and permission filters, then often rerank the top results. The senior caveat is that approximate search needs evaluation and operations work, including backfills, index rebuilds, stale embeddings, and recall or precision measurement.
